security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious votes can alter the governance outcome.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with votes can lead to unexpected governance decisions.

assumptions: The governance contract does not validate the integrity of votes.
The actor can submit votes that manipulate the proposal outcome.

mitigation.strategy: Implement input sanitization and validation for vote data.

description: An attacker could submit manipulated votes to influence the outcome of governance proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be cast without verifiable user identity.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The lack of user identity verification makes it difficult to trace the origin of votes.

assumptions: The voting system does not record user identities with votes.
The actor can cast votes anonymously.

mitigation.strategy: Integrate a secure identity verification mechanism for voting.

description: An attacker could cast votes and deny responsibility for their actions.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be exposed to unauthorized access.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Sensitive vote information could be leaked without proper protection.

assumptions: Vote data is not encrypted during transmission or storage.
The actor can intercept or gain access to vote data.

mitigation.strategy: Encrypt vote data during transmission and storage.

description: An attacker may intercept or gain access to vote data and reveal the voting patterns of users.
